immutable_facts.definition: Path traversal happens when untrusted path input resolves outside the directory the application intended to expose.
immutable_facts.mechanism: Normalize the candidate path, then verify that the normalized result remains strictly below the trusted root.
immutable_facts.analogy_breakpoint: A filesystem path can be crafted byte by byte; a visitor cannot reshape a real corridor.
immutable_facts.verification: A nested note must resolve, while traversal, absolute, empty, non-string, and NUL-containing inputs must be rejected.

confusion_pair.diagnostic: Normalization makes a path canonical; the boundary check decides whether it is allowed.
